steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to introduce the Slow Food movement to a new audience. An effective introduction names the subject, establishes when it began, and summarizes its core mission — giving a reader who knows nothing about the topic enough to understand what it is and why it matters.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The international Slow Food movement, founded in 1989, promotes universal access to healthy, high-quality food that is produced sustainably by workers who are treated and compensated fairly.': Names the movement, gives the founding year, and covers all three core goals (healthy food access, sustainable production, fair worker treatment). A complete, self-contained introduction. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Goals of the movement include universal access to healthy, high-quality food and sustainable food practices.': Never names the movement or gives its founding date, so a new reader has no idea what "the movement" refers to. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The signing of the “Slow Food Manifesto” in 1989 marked the founding of the international Slow Food movement, while the Slow Food USA organization was founded in 2000.': Covers only founding facts for two organizations but says nothing about the movement’s mission or goals. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The Slow Food movement advocates for food production workers.': Mentions only one detail from the notes and omits the name context, founding date, and other core goals. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
